prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for benzaldehyde (14 atoms) at the DFT level using tier 'standard' → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting, plain RKS) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. N±1 states: cation (charge +1, doublet) and anion (charge −1, doublet). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF finite-difference). Geometry from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000. No warnings reported. Headline result: most electrophilic atom is C (atom 3, 1-based, the carbonyl carbon) with f+ = 0.39938968; most nucleophilic atom is O (atom 1, 1-based, the carbonyl oxygen) with f- = 0.23839906.
